questions.question: Same Claude Sonnet 4.5 scored 43.2% on SWE-agent v1 and 59.8% inside Cline. What does that 16.6-point gap demonstrate?
options: Cline used a secretly larger model
Scaffolding — retrieval, verifier loops, sandbox, edit format — is load-bearing; the base model is a component and the loop is the product
SWE-bench scores are random noise
The benchmark favors VS Code extensions
questions.explanation: Identical weights, different scaffolds, 16.6 absolute points. The 4% → 80.9% SWE-bench Verified climb came from compounding model gains AND scaffold gains (CodeAct, reflection, verifier loops) — which is why "which agent is best" is the wrong question.

questions.question: Why are headline SWE-bench Verified scores misleading about real-world reliability?
options: The benchmark is written in a different language
161 of 500 tasks need only 1-2 line changes — on SWE-bench Pro (10+ line tasks) the same frontier systems score 23-59%
Verified scores are self-reported without checks
The tasks are synthetic, not from real repos
questions.explanation: The easy tail pulls scores up and saturation hides regressions. Your production bug backlog is closer to Pro than Verified — so benchmark your own distribution before choosing an agent.

questions.question: What is the CodeAct architecture and its trade-off versus JSON tool calls?
options: CodeAct compiles the model to C; JSON stays interpreted

The model emits Python executed in a sandboxed kernel — compositional, can chain tools and catch exceptions in one action, but the blast radius is everything the sandbox allows; JSON calls are one-auditable-action-per-turn
CodeAct is JSON with comments allowed
JSON tool calls cannot run shell commands
questions.explanation: OpenHands' bet: code-as-action wins on compositionality, at the cost of needing hardened Docker isolation. JSON tool calls dominate managed services where the provider controls the executor; CodeAct dominates open platforms.

questions.question: What are the three scaffolding components that buy the most reliability points?
options: Bigger context, higher temperature, more retries
Retrieval (finding the right files), a verifier loop (run tests, read traces, retry — worth 10+ points), and failure containment (sandbox rollback)
GPU count, RAM, disk speed
Logging, dashboards, alerts
questions.explanation: Coding runs are long-horizon trajectories where reliability compounds per step. Retrieval is the silent bottleneck, the verifier loop is a measured 10+ point delta, and rollback prevents compounding damage.

questions.question: Before adopting a coding agent for your team, what is the right evaluation?
options: Pick the top of the SWE-bench Verified leaderboard
Run a Pro-like subset of your own bug backlog through the candidate scaffold — the score that matters is on tasks representative of what you ship
Choose the one with the most GitHub stars
Benchmark on HumanEval
questions.explanation: End-to-end reliability on your task distribution, with your production scaffolding, is the question. The lesson's audit skill checks retrieval quality, verifier presence, sandbox isolation, and benchmark-to-distribution fit.
